title: Multidimensional arrays should be avoided
description: Multidimensional arrays should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmelldesignWhy is this an issue?A jagged array is an array whose elements are arrays. It is recommended over a multidimensional array because the arrays that make up the elements can be of different sizes, which avoids wasting memory space.Noncompliant code exampleint [,] myArray = // Noncompliant { {1,2,3,4}, {5,6,7,0}, {8,0,0,0}, {9,0,0,0} }; // ... myArray[1,1] = 0;Compliant solutionint[][] myArray = { new int[] {1,2,3,4}, new int[] {5,6,7}, new int[] {8}, new int[] {9} }; // ..
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

logic.reasoning: Detects declarations of multidimensional arrays (containing commas in brackets like [,] or [,,]) that should be replaced with jagged arrays for better memory efficiency and flexibility. Multidimensional arrays waste memory when rows have different lengths and are less flexible than jagged arrays. Excludes test code, logging, interop scenarios, and operations on existing arrays.

should_not_trigger: [Test] public void TestMethod() { int[,] test = new int[2,2]; }
